titel: Minutes from the Monetary Policy Meeting held on June 16, 2026.
html:

Published 2026-06-24

At the monetary policy meeting on June 16, 2026, the board decided to leave the key interest rate unchanged at 1.75 percent.

The minutes of the meeting provide insight into the discussions that led to the decision and show how the different board members argued their positions.

The board typically holds eight monetary policy meetings per year to make decisions about monetary policy. Approximately five business days after each monetary policy meeting, the minutes of the meeting are published.
tldr: The interest rate will remain unchanged. It will stay at 1.75 percent following a decision made on June 16, 2026.
lattlast:
The decision-makers for Sweden's economy, the so-called executive board, held a meeting. During this meeting, they discussed the future direction of interest rates.

The decision was to keep the key interest rate at the same level as before. The key interest rate is the rate that banks pay to the central bank, and it influences how expensive it is for ordinary people to borrow money.

This rate will now remain at 1.75 percent. This means that there is no change in the cost of borrowing at this time.

The executive board holds eight such meetings each year to decide whether to raise, lower, or keep the interest rate unchanged. After each meeting, a record is written detailing the decisions made.
